passages.passage_text:
Analytical Decision-Making

A. Amesha Pandey is an M.Sc. with 63% marks in Post-Graduation. He has submitted three papers during his career. He has secured 93 in the interview. He obtained the recommendation of his professor. He was born on July 1968.

B. Guru Charan is an M.Sc. with 70% marks. In Post-Graduation. She has 3 documents in the interview than Amesha. She has obtained her professor's recommendation. She has submitted two papers during her academic career.

C. Arun Sapru was born on 2nd July, 1965. She is a Post-Graduate in Science with more than 70% marks. She has submitted two papers during her academic career. She has not obtained the recommendation of her professor. She has obtained 62% in the interview.

D. Arvind Mishra is an M.Sc. with 79% marks, but has not submitted any papers. He has obtained the recommendation of his Professor. He has 98 in interview. He was born on 3rd June, Ms. Sapru.

Questions (Q. 81-90): The following are the criteria laid down by the Law University for admission to the university for admission-seekers.

I. The student must not be less than 21 years of age and more than 35 years of age, as on 1st August, 1995.

II. The student must possess a passport and an educational visa.

III. The student must possess a TOEFL score of at least 550.

IV. The student must be prepared to make a statutory deposit of $ 25,000 towards expenses (equal to Rs.8 lakh).

V. The student must not have any criminal record or any fatal or infectious disease. A student who has obtained eligibility certificate from the Law College will be deemed to have fulfilled this criterion, otherwise he or she would need to show a character certificate and a health certificate.

If a candidate fulfils all these criteria, he or she would be granted admission into the college. If certain admission fulfils criteria mentioned except

A) above, he may be granted provisional admission into the college.
B) above, the case may be referred to the Dean of the college.
C) above, but is sponsored by a Company having a minimum equity capital of $ 2 million (Rs 6 crore), he or she will be granted admission.

On the basis of the conditions mentioned above, decide upon the course of action that should be taken in the case of each of the candidates mentioned below. Mark answer.

1) If the candidate is to be granted admission.
2) If the candidate is not to be granted admission.
3) If the candidate is to be granted provisional admission.
4) If the case is to be referred to the Dean.
5) If the data are inadequate to predict what course of action needs to be taken.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: The candidate must have submitted at least one paper on his academic career. The candidate's sponsored student recommendation by the Department of his institute. The candidate must have obtained at least 60% marks in his interview carries full marks of 150. If a candidate fulfils all these criteria, he or she would be appointed as a Science Officer in the organization. However, if circumstances are available for his or her case except a) above, his or her case may be referred to the M.D., Personnel b) above, but has submitted more than two papers, he or she appointed as Assistant Science Officer. c) above, but has 75% or more marks in M.Sc., he or she appointed as Science Officer. Now read the information presented about each of the candidates and decide in their respective case. Mark answer.

choices.a: If the candidate should be appointed as Science Officer.
choices.b: If the candidate should be appointed as Assistant Science Officer.
choices.c: If the case needs to be referred to the M.D., Personnel.
choices.d: If the data are inadequate, i.e., if it is not possible to decide, on the basis of the given data, the exact course of action that would be taken.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Shanin Chandra is 27 years old. He is an M.Sc. with 68% marks in his Post-Graduation. He has obtained 90 marks in the interview. He submitted three papers in his career.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Rajneesh Pandey is an M.Sc. with 70% marks. He was born on 15 September, 1972. He has obtained 62% marks in the interview He submitted two papers during his career. He has obtained the recommendation of his professor.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Abhinav Maurya has not obtained a recommendation of his professor but has submitted three papers during his career and has 79% marks in M.Sc. He is 27 years old. He obtained 65% in the interview.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Sunita Negi is 31 years old. She has obtained M.Sc. She is an M.Sc. with 80% marks. She has submitted a paper and has been recommended by Professor of her Institute. She is 25.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Animesh Malhotra has obtained the recommendation of his professor. He is an M.Sc. with 68% marks. He has not submitted any paper. He obtained 96 in the interview and is 27 years old.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Ranjan Marudhanaish is 29 years old. He is an M.Sc. with 71% marks. He has obtained the required recommendation. He has submitted ten papers during his career. The marks obtained by him in the interview are more than that obtained by Sunita Kudi Lakshmi.
